free_text: Study of the 25Mg(d,p)26Mg reaction at Ed = 8.0 MeV
1.55 mg/cm2 thick target.
The detector was a 3 mm thick, lithium-drifted
silicon, solid-state detector.
A beam of 8 MeV deuterons from the Livermore variable-
energy cyclotron was collimated so that it had a
circular spot size with a 0.32 cm diam. at the target.
The beam was monitored by means of a Faraday cup and
a second detector kept fixed at 90 degrees with
respect to the beam. In order to detect only protons
from the 25Mg(d,p)26Mg reaction, use was made of the
high positive Q-value for the reaction (Q = +8.89
MeV) and a gold foil (196 mg/cm2 thick) was placed
between the target and detector. The protons passed
through this foil, but all other charged particles
were stopped. Most of the data presented in this
report were obtained with the gold foil directly in
front of a collimator with a 0.48 cm diam. hole
placed 7.6 cm in front of the collimator which
defined the solid angle of the detector. Finally,
some runs were made with no gold foil. This made it
possible to obtain the deuteron elastic scattering data
and set the absolute normalization of the proton data.
